title: poa pratensis var. rigens
intro: Poa pratensis var. rigens, also known as tall bluegrass or green meadow-grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Western Asia, and North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been employed to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of Poa pratensis var. rigens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Poa pratensis var. rigens can be made by infusing the dried grass in hot water or by using it in the form of tea, tincture, or decoction, and are often used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
